extracted_information: The web content focuses extensively on SaaS churn, providing definitions, calculation methods, types of churn metrics (Subscriber, MRR, Gross MRR, Net MRR), and their importance for revenue tracking, growth forecasting, and identifying areas for improvement. It also delves into common reasons for customer churn and methods for prediction, implicitly forming the basis for retention strategies.

specifications.saas_churn_definition: The percentage rate at which customers of a SaaS solution cancel their recurring subscriptions.
specifications.saas_churn_calculation_simple: Number of customers canceling their SaaS subscription in a set time interval / Number of subscribers at the start of the set time interval.

churn_metrics_types.name: Subscriber Churn Rate (Logo Churn)
churn_metrics_types.definition: The rate at which your subscribers are canceling their subscriptions.
churn_metrics_types.calculation: Subscriber Churn Count / Start of Period Subscriber Count
churn_metrics_types.example: If 200 subscribers at start, 10 churned, Rate = 5%.
churn_metrics_types.value: Valuable for early-stage startups, ties to customer satisfaction, used for churn forecasting (probability rate of cancellation).

churn_metrics_types.name: MRR Churn Rate
churn_metrics_types.definition: The rate at which monthly recurring revenue (MRR) is lost from canceled subscriptions of your existing paying customer base (excluding new business).
churn_metrics_types.calculation: Churn MRR / Start of Period MRR
churn_metrics_types.value: Financial metric showing impact of churn on revenue. Helps analyze if lower or higher MRR customers are churning. Can forecast future revenue performance and acts as an early-warning system.

churn_metrics_types.name: Gross MRR Churn
churn_metrics_types.definition: The sum of MRR lost from both canceled subscriptions and downgrades (contraction).
churn_metrics_types.rate_calculation: (Churn MRR + Contraction MRR) / Start of Period MRR
churn_metrics_types.value: Considered a more 'honest' look at business health as it doesn't include expansion MRR. Low Gross MRR Churn indicates a healthy business and is a metric investors pay attention to.

churn_metrics_types.name: Net MRR Churn
churn_metrics_types.definition: The sum of MRR lost from canceled subscriptions and contraction PLUS MRR gained from expansion (upgrades) and reactivation.
churn_metrics_types.calculation: (Churn MRR + Contraction MRR) - (Expansion MRR + Reactivation MRR)
churn_metrics_types.rate_calculation: Net MRR Churn / Start of Period MRR
churn_metrics_types.value: Combines unhappy (churn/contraction) and happy (expansion/reactivation) subscribers, providing a full picture. Includes cross-sells, up-sells, organic growth within customer accounts, and price increases. Tells the story of what would happen to a SaaS business over time without acquiring new customers.

churn_metrics_types.name: Negative Churn
churn_metrics_types.definition: A state where the value of your existing customer base is growing even without factoring in any new business. It's the only churn rate metric that can be negative.
churn_metrics_types.example: Company loses 5% of customers monthly, but remaining 95% grow spend by 10%, leading to total revenue of 105% from the previous month for that cohort.

specifications.saas_retention_rate_good: Between 80% and 85% for an average market. Higher target audience implies higher retention rate.

pricing.price_as_churn_reason: One of the leading causes of SaaS churn if quality doesn't match price point or strategic pricing plan is missing.

items: Price: Quality of service not matching price point, or lack of strategic pricing.
Product: Failure to deliver on expectations or marketed features.
Competition: Customers finding comparable or better solutions.
Poor Experiences: Difficult onboarding, negative customer service interactions.
Leveraging Customer Success data: Retrospectives and ongoing meetings.
Analyzing service tickets: Insight into general user sentiment.
Customer satisfaction surveys: To gauge likelihood of finding alternatives.
Analyzing previous months' SaaS churn metrics: To predict future churn and serve as benchmarks.
MRR Churn Rate shows financial impact on revenue.
Gross MRR Churn indicates business health and is important for investors.
Net MRR Churn provides a complete picture of revenue changes from existing customers (churn, contraction, expansion, reactivation).
Understanding churn rate allows for better calculation of expected monthly cash flow for reinvestment and overhead planning.

temporal_info.impact_period_for_churn_reasons: Just one poor customer experience can lead to non-renewal.
temporal_info.churn_rate_variation: Varies significantly between different industries and subscription tiers.

references.author: Steli Efti
references.title: CEO of Close.io
quotes: Just because customer churn is simple doesn’t mean it’s not useful. It’s probably the only measurement a startup needs to address retention in its infancy.

If you charge per user or have different pricing tiers, revenue churn lets you put a dollar amount on what churn is actually costing you, and at what velocity. Revenue churn can be drastically different than customer churn, and will often tell a completely different story.

references.author: Joel York
references.title: Founder and CEO of Markodojo
quotes: When used in forecasting, SaaS [subscriber] churn can be interpreted as the probability rate at which customers will cancel their subscriptions.

references.author: Alexander Bruehl
references.title: Founder of SaaS Garage
quotes: Lots of churn definitions out there. It all boils down to a low Gross MRR Churn indicating if a business is healthy. Net MRR can be ‘improved’ by expansion, but if the Gross MRR Churn is above 1-2% there seems to be an issue with the product or the ROI story.

references.author: Nathan Latka
references.title: CEO of Founderpath
quotes: The quickest way to build your SaaS company valuation is to keep current customers paying for 50 months (means 2% or less gross monthly churn) and drive expansion revenue from current customer base (negative net monthly churn).

references.author: Todd Gardner
references.title: founder of SaaS Capital
quotes: Because Net MRR Churn metrics include “cross-sells, up-sells, organic growth within a customer account, and price increases” they’re important as broader metrics “which tell the story of what would happen to a SaaS business over time if it did not acquire any new customers.”

references.author: Tomasz Tunguz
references.title: Managing Director at Redpoint Ventures
quotes: The company may lose “5% of its customer base each month, but the remaining 95% of the customers grow their spend with the startup by 10 percentage points, so the total revenue from the cohort is equal to 105% of the revenue from the previous month. Like a savings account, each month, every cohort becomes more valuable.”

references.author: Jesse Pärnänen
references.title: Business Development & Partnerships Manager at Leadfeeder
quotes: Even if you’ve achieved negative churn or a low net churn, there is always room for improvement “because gross churn can be a more accurate measurement of how your SaaS tool and customer success team is working.”
